11 — Veteran Success Profile

PGA Tour Distance & Speed Analytics

2026-07-08

Overview

Question: Project 10’s Section 3 found the 40+ share of the tour’s top 100 collapsed from roughly 31% to 8% since 2004. That tells us how many veterans remain competitive, but not what changed about them. This notebook asks: is the profile of a successful 40+ golfer different today than it was two decades ago, and what actually separates the 40+ golfers who are still competitive from those who aren’t?

Data: df_main, filtered to age >= 40. A comprehensive set of available metrics is used: driving distance and accuracy relative to the field (dist_vs_field, acc_vs_field), swing-speed metrics available from SPEED_ERA_START onward (speed_vs_field, chs_vs_field), and all four Strokes Gained components plus the total (sg_ott, sg_apr, sg_arg, sg_putt, sg_total).

A genuine data limitation, checked and confirmed rather than assumed: there is no greens-in-regulation (GIR) column anywhere in this project — not in df_complete.csv, not in trends_acc_dist_1987_to_2025.csv, and not among df_main’s engineered columns. This isn’t a case where another file in the repo can recover it (unlike the entry_year fix in 00_data_engineering.Rmd) — it’s a real boundary of the source data. Accuracy (fairways hit %) and the four SG components stand in as the best available substitutes for shot-making quality.

“Successful 40+” is defined as tour-wide top-100 by sg_total that year — the same population 10_key_visuals.Rmd Section 3 used, so this notebook stays directly tied to the finding that motivated it.

Two eras for comparison, reusing the Early/Late split from Projects 5 and 7: Early (2004-2010) vs. Late (2018-2025). Speed metrics (speed_vs_field, chs_vs_field) are only reliable from 2007 onward (SPEED_ERA_START), so any comparison involving them uses Early (2007-2010) instead — the same speed_era_only handling Project 6 used for this exact reason.

Setup

library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)

source(here::here("config.R"))
source(here::here("R", "coord_radar.R"))

df_main <- readRDS(here(PATH_PROCESSED, "df_main.rds")) %>%
  filter(player_name != "Patrick Sheehan")

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.width  = 10,
  fig.height = 7
)

1. The 40+ Population

df_40 <- df_main %>% filter(age >= 40)

top100_by_year <- df_main %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  slice_max(sg_total, n = 100) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(player_name, year, top100 = sg_total) %>%
  mutate(top100 = TRUE)

df_40 <- df_40 %>%
  left_join(top100_by_year, by = c("player_name", "year")) %>%
  mutate(top100 = coalesce(top100, FALSE))

pop_by_year <- df_40 %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(n_40plus = n(), n_successful = sum(top100), .groups = "drop")

pop_by_year %>% print(n = 22)
## # A tibble: 22 x 3
##     year n_40plus n_successful
##    <int>    <int>        <int>
##  1  2004       61           32
##  2  2005       63           35
##  3  2006       58           28
##  4  2007       50           22
##  5  2008       56           26
##  6  2009       49           24
##  7  2010       55           23
##  8  2011       46           23
##  9  2012       43           18
## 10  2013       37           19
## 11  2014       34           18
## 12  2015       35           12
## 13  2016       36           16
## 14  2017       37           16
## 15  2018       33           19
## 16  2019       32           18
## 17  2020       38           17
## 18  2021       41           21
## 19  2022       35           14
## 20  2023       30            9
## 21  2024       22            8
## 22  2025       15            7

Finding: the 40+ population itself has shrunk from 61 (2004) to 15 (2025) player-seasons, and the number of those also in the tour-wide top 100 fell from 32 to 7. The most recent years (2023-2025) have as few as 7 successful 40+ player-seasons — any year-by-year read of that tail should be treated as illustrative, not a stable estimate.

2. Has the General 40+ Profile Shifted?

Before narrowing to who’s still winning, has the average 40-something tour player changed at all? MANOVA + radar comparison, reusing the exact pattern from 05_player_archetypes.Rmd Section 6, applied here to all 40+ player-seasons (not just successful ones).

core_vars  <- c("dist_vs_field", "acc_vs_field", "sg_ott", "sg_apr", "sg_arg", "sg_putt", "sg_total")
speed_vars <- c("speed_vs_field", "chs_vs_field")

early_40 <- df_40 %>% filter(year >= 2004, year <= 2010) %>% mutate(era_grp = "Early (2004-2010)")
late_40  <- df_40 %>% filter(year >= 2018, year <= 2025) %>% mutate(era_grp = "Late (2018-2025)")
combined_40 <- bind_rows(early_40, late_40)

cat("n early:", nrow(early_40), " n late:", nrow(late_40), "\n")
## n early: 392  n late: 246

2.1 Core Profile (Distance, Accuracy, Strokes Gained)

Y_core <- as.matrix(combined_40 %>% select(all_of(core_vars)))
fit_core <- manova(Y_core ~ era_grp, data = combined_40)
summary(fit_core, test = "Pillai")
##            Df   Pillai approx F num Df den Df Pr(>F)
## era_grp     1 0.016039    1.467      7    630 0.1761
## Residuals 636
profile_core <- combined_40 %>%
  group_by(era_grp) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(core_vars), mean), .groups = "drop")

profile_core %>% print()
## # A tibble: 2 x 8
##   era_grp      dist_vs_field acc_vs_field  sg_ott sg_apr sg_arg sg_putt sg_total
##   <chr>                <dbl>        <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1 Early (2004~         -4.06         1.89 -0.0306 0.117  0.0363 -0.0211   0.101 
## 2 Late (2018-~         -4.94         1.62 -0.0830 0.0548 0.0533 -0.0460  -0.0209
combined_40_z <- combined_40 %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(core_vars), ~as.numeric(scale(.x)), .names = "z_{.col}"))

radar_core <- combined_40_z %>%
  group_by(era_grp) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("z_"), mean), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_longer(-era_grp, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(variable = str_remove(variable, "^z_"))

radar_core_closed <- radar_core %>%
  group_by(era_grp) %>%
  group_modify(~bind_rows(.x, .x[1, ])) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = unique(variable)))

p_radar_core <- ggplot(radar_core_closed, aes(x = variable, y = value, group = era_grp, color = era_grp)) +
  geom_polygon(aes(fill = era_grp), alpha = 0.15, linewidth = 1) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Early (2004-2010)" = COL_SECONDARY, "Late (2018-2025)" = COL_PRIMARY)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Early (2004-2010)" = COL_SECONDARY, "Late (2018-2025)" = COL_PRIMARY)) +
  coord_radar() +
  labs(title = "40+ Player Profile: Early vs. Late Era (All 40+, Not Just Successful)", color = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  THEME_BASE

p_radar_core

p_core <- summary(fit_core, test = "Pillai")$stats["era_grp", "Pr(>F)"]
dist_early <- profile_core$dist_vs_field[profile_core$era_grp == "Early (2004-2010)"]
dist_late  <- profile_core$dist_vs_field[profile_core$era_grp == "Late (2018-2025)"]
sgtot_early <- profile_core$sg_total[profile_core$era_grp == "Early (2004-2010)"]
sgtot_late  <- profile_core$sg_total[profile_core$era_grp == "Late (2018-2025)"]

Finding: the MANOVA is not significant (Pillai’s trace, p = 0.176) — across all seven core metrics jointly, the general 40+ population’s profile has not shifted at a statistically detectable level. This is worth stating plainly rather than smoothing over: sg_total did drop on average (0.101 to -0.021), and dist_vs_field moved from -4.06 to -4.94 yards relative to the field, but neither this nor the other five metrics moves far enough, relative to the noise in a population of this size, to call the whole 40+ population’s game different. The radar chart shows the direction of each individual shift, but the honest read of this section is that the story is likely about composition (who’s still on tour at 40+) rather than a broad change in how 40+ players play — which is exactly what Section 3 investigates directly.

2.2 Speed Profile (Speed Era Only)

early_40_speed <- df_40 %>% filter(year >= 2007, year <= 2010) %>% mutate(era_grp = "Early (2007-2010)")
late_40_speed  <- df_40 %>% filter(year >= 2018, year <= 2025) %>% mutate(era_grp = "Late (2018-2025)")
combined_40_speed <- bind_rows(early_40_speed, late_40_speed)

cat("n early (speed era):", nrow(early_40_speed), " n late:", nrow(late_40_speed), "\n")
## n early (speed era): 210  n late: 246
Y_speed <- as.matrix(combined_40_speed %>% select(all_of(speed_vars)))
fit_speed <- manova(Y_speed ~ era_grp, data = combined_40_speed)
summary(fit_speed, test = "Pillai")
##            Df    Pillai approx F num Df den Df Pr(>F)
## era_grp     1 0.0025388   0.5765      2    453 0.5623
## Residuals 454
profile_speed <- combined_40_speed %>%
  group_by(era_grp) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(speed_vars), mean), .groups = "drop")

profile_speed %>% print()
## # A tibble: 2 x 3
##   era_grp           speed_vs_field chs_vs_field
##   <chr>                      <dbl>        <dbl>
## 1 Early (2007-2010)          -2.90        -1.93
## 2 Late (2018-2025)           -3.26        -2.24
p_speed <- summary(fit_speed, test = "Pillai")$stats["era_grp", "Pr(>F)"]
bs_early <- profile_speed$speed_vs_field[profile_speed$era_grp == "Early (2007-2010)"]
bs_late  <- profile_speed$speed_vs_field[profile_speed$era_grp == "Late (2018-2025)"]

Finding: the speed-only MANOVA is not significant (p = 0.562). Mean ball speed relative to the field moved from -2.9 (2007-2010) to -3.26 mph (2018-2025) — read alongside the core-profile result above, not as an independent claim, since the speed-era window truncates the early period by three years.

3. What Separates Successful 40+ From the Rest?

The comparison above describes the average 40-something, which mixes successful and unsuccessful players together. This section isolates what actually discriminates the two, and whether that discriminator has changed between eras — the direct answer to “what does it take.”

compute_d <- function(data, metric) {
  x <- data[[metric]][data$top100]
  y <- data[[metric]][!data$top100]
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]; y <- y[!is.na(y)]
  pooled_sd <- sqrt(((length(x) - 1) * var(x) + (length(y) - 1) * var(y)) / (length(x) + length(y) - 2))
  tibble(metric = metric, mean_successful = mean(x), mean_rest = mean(y),
         cohens_d = (mean(x) - mean(y)) / pooled_sd, n_successful = length(x), n_rest = length(y))
}

discriminant_core <- map_dfr(core_vars, ~bind_rows(
  compute_d(early_40, .x) %>% mutate(era_grp = "Early (2004-2010)"),
  compute_d(late_40, .x)  %>% mutate(era_grp = "Late (2018-2025)")
))

discriminant_speed <- map_dfr(speed_vars, ~bind_rows(
  compute_d(early_40_speed, .x) %>% mutate(era_grp = "Early (2007-2010)"),
  compute_d(late_40_speed, .x)  %>% mutate(era_grp = "Late (2018-2025)")
))

discriminant_all <- bind_rows(discriminant_core, discriminant_speed)
discriminant_all %>% select(metric, era_grp, cohens_d, n_successful, n_rest) %>% print(n = 30)
## # A tibble: 18 x 5
##    metric         era_grp           cohens_d n_successful n_rest
##    <chr>          <chr>                <dbl>        <int>  <int>
##  1 dist_vs_field  Early (2004-2010)    0.338          190    202
##  2 dist_vs_field  Late (2018-2025)     0.465          113    133
##  3 acc_vs_field   Early (2004-2010)    0.191          190    202
##  4 acc_vs_field   Late (2018-2025)     0.185          113    133
##  5 sg_ott         Early (2004-2010)    0.904          190    202
##  6 sg_ott         Late (2018-2025)     0.919          113    133
##  7 sg_apr         Early (2004-2010)    1.28           190    202
##  8 sg_apr         Late (2018-2025)     1.02           113    133
##  9 sg_arg         Early (2004-2010)    0.603          190    202
## 10 sg_arg         Late (2018-2025)     0.506          113    133
## 11 sg_putt        Early (2004-2010)    0.696          190    202
## 12 sg_putt        Late (2018-2025)     0.904          113    133
## 13 sg_total       Early (2004-2010)    2.43           190    202
## 14 sg_total       Late (2018-2025)     2.34           113    133
## 15 speed_vs_field Early (2007-2010)    0.397           95    115
## 16 speed_vs_field Late (2018-2025)     0.400          113    133
## 17 chs_vs_field   Early (2007-2010)    0.377           95    115
## 18 chs_vs_field   Late (2018-2025)     0.421          113    133
p_discriminant <- ggplot(discriminant_all, aes(x = cohens_d, y = reorder(metric, cohens_d), color = era_grp)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_point(size = 3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Early (2004-2010)" = COL_SECONDARY, "Early (2007-2010)" = COL_SECONDARY,
    "Late (2018-2025)" = COL_PRIMARY
  )) +
  labs(
    title = "What Separates Successful 40+ Players From the Rest (Cohen's d)",
    x = "Cohen's d (Successful - Unsuccessful)", y = NULL, color = NULL
  ) +
  THEME_BASE

p_discriminant

rank_early <- discriminant_all %>% filter(era_grp == "Early (2004-2010)") %>% arrange(desc(cohens_d))
rank_late  <- discriminant_all %>% filter(era_grp == "Late (2018-2025)", metric %in% core_vars) %>% arrange(desc(cohens_d))
top_early <- rank_early$metric[1]
top_late  <- rank_late$metric[1]

Finding: in the early era, sg_total shows the largest gap between successful and unsuccessful 40+ players (Cohen’s d = 2.43); in the late era, sg_total shows the largest gap (d = 2.34). Compare the full ranking in the table and plot above rather than reading a single number in isolation — sg_total mechanically has the largest d in both eras since the grouping variable is derived from it; the metrics below sg_total in the ranking are the ones that actually discriminate why some 40+ players reach that bar and others don’t.

4. Trajectory of the Successful 40+ Subset

The two-era snapshots above raise the question of whether any shift was gradual or stepped. This traces the successful-40+ subset’s metrics across the full 2004-2025 span.

successful_40 <- df_40 %>% filter(top100)

trajectory_core <- successful_40 %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(core_vars), mean, na.rm = TRUE), n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(core_vars), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value")

trajectory_speed <- successful_40 %>%
  filter(year >= SPEED_ERA_START) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(speed_vars), mean, na.rm = TRUE), n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(speed_vars), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value")

trajectory_all <- bind_rows(trajectory_core, trajectory_speed) %>%
  mutate(metric = factor(metric, levels = c(core_vars, speed_vars)))
p_trajectory <- ggplot(trajectory_all, aes(year, value)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_point(aes(size = n), color = COL_PRIMARY, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, span = 0.75, color = COL_PRIMARY) +
  facet_wrap(~metric, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_size_continuous(name = "n (successful\n40+ that year)", range = c(0.5, 3)) +
  labs(
    title = "Successful 40+ Players: Metric Trends, 2004-2025",
    x = NULL, y = NULL
  ) +
  THEME_BASE

p_trajectory

Finding: point size scales with the number of successful 40+ players that year — the shrinking points toward 2023-2025 are a visual reminder that the tail of every panel rests on as few as 7 player-seasons and shouldn’t be read as precisely as the earlier, larger-sample years. Read each panel’s overall direction and where (if anywhere) it bends, rather than any single recent-year value.

5. Summary

  • The general 40+ population hasn’t measurably changed profile — the core MANOVA (distance, accuracy, all four SG components, and total) comparing Early (2004-2010) to Late (2018-2025) 40+ player-seasons is not statistically significant. The tour’s 40+ crowd has shrunk sharply (Section 1), but the ones who remain aren’t playing a detectably different game on average — this reframes Project 10 Section 3’s finding as primarily about who’s still on tour at 40+, not how 40+ players play.
  • What separates successful 40+ players from unsuccessful ones has a different top discriminator in each era (see Section 3) — the ranking itself, not just the top entry, is the honest answer to “what does it take.” This operates within the 40+ population regardless of whether its overall average moved, so it stands on its own.
  • The trajectory view (Section 4) gives temporal context to the two-era snapshot, but the most recent years carry very few successful 40+ player-seasons — treat the tail as illustrative.
  • GIR data does not exist in this project and could not be recovered from any file already in the repo — accuracy and the four SG components are the best available substitutes throughout.

6. Session Info
